item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It kinda looks like there was a different 3896 that maybe got taken down?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is it just me, or does the link to 3896 not make sense in the context given?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What is this, an echo chamber? Have you guys all forgotten how series 3's are??

I like this a lot! And, forget what they say, cuz series 3 SCPs often have tons of associated links and other tales needed to piece together the full story. You link to everything needed, you can find everything needed right on this page. +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While I like the ideas presented here, and the intro did a great job of catching my attention, I can’t quite figure out what’s going on here. As such, I have to downvote, because to a new reader this is a virtually impenetrable fog of references and allusions.

That being said, the ideas that I did tease out were fascinating, and I’d be glad to change to an upvote if this can be made a bit more newcomer-friendly. Perhaps add a few tabs: one with an overview for low-level people not cleared to know everything, then increasing levels of security, up to this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly, I don't mind the interconnection with other scips. In fact, I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: He included links to most all the others you'd need to read to fully understand this one, you just have to be willing to work for it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This should be a tale or hub, not a SCiP of its own.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed reading this, but most part of it was to me not understandable, not because of the language, but because the article was written with no explanation of the previous.
I'm afraid I have to say it might be unfriendly to new users, or users that have read about less than 1000 random SCPs (like me).

If there is something I think needed to be changed here, is simply:

Act as if every SCP will be the first that someone will read. That means do not put too much in there that requires knowledge of anything else on the site.

How To Write An SCP

Still it is very well written and I enjoyed the language and the indestructible MTF-Sigma-01 Force concept.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm a new reader; I haven't read any of the others in the series. I do not believe this SCP stands on its own. I was intrigued by the initial message from the commander of the MTF, but once I got to the SCP, I lost interest. I had no idea what was going on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Tentative upvote, though I have my reservations. Specifically, can this stand on its own? Can someone come to this without having read any of the other skips in this series and get what's going on? Care about it? I saw "I accept the burden" and was psyched, but the interconnection of these articles is really strong, and I'm not sure this functions as anything more than payoff for avid readers.

item.title: HYPE
item.content:encoded:
The board is set. The rules have been read. The pieces are in position. The wagers have been made.

Now let's play.

item.title: welp
item.content:encoded:
my eighth item, to address and combat my ninth.

a big thank you to everyone that helped me with this. it would not exist without you all.

and thank you, reader, for sticking with me. ♥
